chimera-monitor-cfn-polling: chimera monitor command uses cf-monitor utility (monitorStack + findActiveStacks). monitorStack polls DescribeStackEvents every 10s, deduplicates via seenEventIds Set, prints events chronologically (reverse the newest-first page), and checks terminal state via DescribeStacks. showHistory=false seeds seenEventIds on start to skip historical events. no-constant-condition eslint rule requires eslint-disable comment on while(true) polling loops.

cli-ux-audit-findings: CLI has 19 identified UX issues across 10 dimensions. Critical: hardcoded us-east-1 fallback (convention violation), debug output leaking to stderr, skill uninstall --force dead code. High: no shell completions, flat --help with no command grouping, missing --json/--region on several commands, api-client has no timeout. Medium: no usage examples in help, inconsistent error format, no --verbose flag, doctor only checks 5/11 stacks.

name: cli-ux-review-methodology
description: cli-ux-review-methodology: Effective CLI UX audit pattern: read all command files + shared utilities (color, output, workspace, api-client), then systematically check 10 dimensions. Cross-cutting findings like region fallback emerge from comparing all commands against conventions.

content: deploy-zero-touch-flow: chimera deploy now auto-runs endpoints+setup+login after a successful deploy. Uses provisionAdminUser() from setup.ts and terminalLogin() from login.ts. Auto-endpoints skips silently when stacks not yet deployed (fresh deploy case). --no-setup skips Cognito provisioning; --skip-setup-prompt auto-generates password; --monitor polls CloudFormation events every 10s.

deploy-monitor-flag: --monitor on chimera deploy polls DescribeStackEvents every 10s until terminal status (CREATE_COMPLETE/FAILED/ROLLBACK). Shows oldest-first events with color-coded status. Without --monitor on fresh deploys, prints 'chimera monitor' guidance.

cli-region-no-hardcode-pattern: All CLI commands must error with 'No AWS region configured. Run chimera init to set up your workspace.' when region is absent — never fall back to us-east-1. Pattern: remove ?? 'us-east-1', add if (\!region) check with JSON and spinner/console error variants. Commands with --region flag check options.region ?? wsConfig?.aws?.region; commands without just check wsConfig?.aws?.region.

cli-doctor-stack-list-source-of-truth: CHIMERA_STACK_SUFFIXES in doctor.ts must stay in sync with STACK_DESTROY_ORDER in destroy.ts. Both lists define the canonical 11 deployed stacks. When new stacks are added to infra/bin/chimera.ts they must also be added to both destroy.ts and doctor.ts.

cli-command-error-ux: CLI command catch blocks must use console.error(error.message) + process.exit(1) instead of throw error, to prevent raw stack traces reaching users. Pattern: spinner.fail(msg) then console.error + process.exit(1)
api-client-debug-gating: All process.stderr.write debug lines in api-client.ts gated behind process.env.CHIMERA_DEBUG. Normal users never see debug output; set CHIMERA_DEBUG=1 to enable.

api-client-fetch-timeout: request() and requestStream() use AbortController with 30s timeout. On abort throws ChimeraTimeoutError('Connection timed out...'). clearTimeout() in finally block prevents timer leak.

cli-guardAuth-preflight: guardAuth() in api-client.ts throws ChimeraAuthError before any network call. Commands call it at the top of each try block. Ensures clean auth error message rather than opaque API failure.

cli-deploy-zero-touch-flow: chimera deploy auto-runs endpoints+setup+login after successful deployment. ensureAdminCredentials() prompts for password (enter/generate/skip), provisionAdminUser() creates Cognito user, terminalLogin() authenticates. Flags: --no-setup skips, --skip-setup-prompt auto-generates. Dynamic next-steps shows only remaining actions.

cli-destroy-codecommit-cleanup: chimera destroy deletes CodeCommit repo via SDK after CDK stack teardown. --keep-repo flag preserves repo. CodeCommit repo is not CDK-managed (created by deploy.ts SDK), so CDK destroy leaves it orphaned — explicit DeleteRepositoryCommand required.

cli-monitor-command: chimera monitor polls DescribeStackEvents every N seconds (default 10s) for all active Chimera-{env}-* stacks. cf-monitor.ts exports monitorStack() (singular) + findActiveStacks(). Color-coded: green=COMPLETE, yellow=IN_PROGRESS, red=FAILED/ROLLBACK. Sequential stack monitoring with summary. Both deploy and destroy have inline monitorStackEvents() for --monitor flag.

name: cli-parallel-builder-shared-interface
description: When 3 builders work in parallel with a shared utility (cf-monitor.ts), define the function signature in all specs. Accept that builders may deviate from the spec signature (monitorStack vs monitorStacks). Both deploy.ts and destroy.ts implemented inline monitoring rather than importing the shared util — acceptable because their use case differs from standalone monitoring.

content: cli-region-no-fallback-error: All CLI commands must error when region is not configured instead of silently falling back to us-east-1. Pattern: const region = options.region ?? wsConfig?.aws?.region; if (\!region) { error('Region not configured. Run chimera init or pass --region.'); } This applies to deploy, destroy, setup, status, login, connect commands.

cli-api-client-debug-gating: api-client.ts debug stderr (process.stderr.write) must be gated behind process.env.CHIMERA_DEBUG. Default is silent. This prevents confusing debug output from reaching end users.

cli-guardAuth-preflight: session/skill/tenant commands call guardAuth() at the start of each action handler. guardAuth() checks for valid, non-expired access_token in ~/.chimera/credentials. Fails fast with clean error instead of deep stack trace.

cli-error-ux-coverage-complete: All 15 CLI command action handlers use spinner.fail+console.error(error.message)+process.exit(1) in top-level catch blocks. Inner helper functions may use throw (propagates to outer catch). No spinner.fail+throw or bare throw patterns remain in action handlers. Coverage: session/skill/tenant fixed by builder-error-ux; connect/deploy/destroy/monitor/setup/status/sync/upgrade/doctor/login/chat/init/upgrade were already compliant.
cli-verbose-debug-flag: chimera CLI has --verbose and --debug global options on root program; preAction hook sets process.env.CHIMERA_DEBUG='1' to activate debug output in api-client.ts

cli-compiled-version-define: Compiled chimera binaries embed version via bun build --define '__CHIMERA_VERSION__="x.y.z"'. getVersion() checks typeof __CHIMERA_VERSION__ \!== 'undefined' first, then falls back to reading package.json via import.meta.dir in dev mode

cli-exitoverride-try-catch: program.exitOverride() makes Commander throw CommanderError instead of calling process.exit(). Wrap program.parse() in try/catch: code 'commander.helpDisplayed'/'commander.version' → exit(0), all others → exit(err.exitCode ?? 2)

doctor-toml-schema-toolchain: doctor.ts checkTomlSchema() validates [aws] region + [workspace] environment fields; checkToolchain() uses execFileSync (not execSync/exec) for bun/npx/aws detection to satisfy security hook that blocks shell-based exec patterns

aws-errors-offline-detection: isOfflineError() in utils/aws-errors.ts checks both NodeJS.ErrnoException.code (ENOTFOUND/ETIMEDOUT/ECONNREFUSED/ECONNRESET/ENETUNREACH/EAI_AGAIN) and error message strings (AWS SDK wraps OS errors). checkApiConnectivity preserves raw code in detail string for test compatibility while appending human hint

cli-verbose-debug-flag: chimera CLI --verbose/--debug global flag uses Commander preAction hook to set CHIMERA_DEBUG=1 before any command runs. api-client.ts debug output, AWS SDK logger, and verbose stack traces all gate on this env var.

cli-compiled-version-define: Compiled binaries get version via bun build --define __CHIMERA_VERSION__. getVersion() checks typeof __CHIMERA_VERSION__ first, falls back to import.meta.dir + package.json for dev mode. Compile scripts in package.json hardcode the version string.

cli-exit-code-semantics: program.exitOverride() + try/catch around program.parse(). CommanderError with code commander.helpDisplayed or commander.version exits 0. Other CommanderError uses err.exitCode (typically 2 for usage errors). Non-Commander errors exit 1.

cli-offline-error-detection: aws-errors.ts isOfflineError() checks both ErrnoException.code (ENOTFOUND, ETIMEDOUT, ECONNREFUSED, ECONNRESET, ENETUNREACH, EAI_AGAIN) and message substring (AWS SDK wraps network errors in message). Integrated into api-client.ts request/requestStream and doctor.ts checkStackStatus/checkApiConnectivity.

cli-doctor-7-checks: chimera doctor runs 7 checks: (1) AWS credentials, (2) Chimera auth, (3) API connectivity, (4) Cognito config, (5) CloudFormation stacks, (6) chimera.toml schema (aws.region + workspace.environment required), (7) Toolchain (bun, npx, aws via execFileSync).

cli-skill-uninstall-force-dead-code: skill uninstall --force in skill.ts was dead code — the confirmation check must run BEFORE spinner.start(). Always confirm before async operations, not after starting a spinner.

cli-color-dim-vs-gray: Use color.dim (ANSI 2 = faint) for secondary/informational text, not color.gray (ANSI 90 = bright black). color.dim is semantically correct for progress messages and hints. Standardized across sync.ts, upgrade.ts, tenant.ts.

cli-json-output-pattern: Commands with --json must: (1) stop spinner before JSON output, (2) output {status:'ok'|'error', data|error, code} structure, (3) suppress all interactive prompts, (4) read back saved credentials after auth to include expires_at in response.

cli-region-override-pattern: Commands using AWS SDK read region as: options.region ?? wsConfig?.aws?.region. If neither exists, error with 'No AWS region configured. Run chimera init to configure your workspace.' Consistent with doctor.ts pattern.

cli-color-standard: All CLI secondary/hint text must use color.gray (ANSI 90, bright-black) not color.dim (ANSI 2, dim attribute). color.gray is the canonical style for de-emphasized output in chimera CLI commands.

cherry-pick-selective-hunk: When a builder branch has desired features mixed with destructive changes, apply changes file-by-file manually rather than using git cherry-pick. Use git diff <main> <branch> -- <file> to inspect each file's changes, then edit target files to add only desired hunks.

cli-completion-command: chimera completion bash|zsh|fish generates shell tab-completion scripts. registerCompletionCommand lives in packages/cli/src/commands/completion.ts. COMMAND_GROUPS and configureHelp override live in cli.ts for grouped help output (Setup/Operations/Auth/Data/Diagnostic sections).

cli-help-grouping-configureHelp: program.configureHelp({ formatHelp }) in cli.ts groups help into sections (Setup/Operations/Auth/Agent/Admin/Diagnostic) using COMMAND_GROUPS map. Commander._hidden=true hides deprecated 'connect' alias (no public hideHelp on Command). Use str.length not helper.displayWidth (doesn't exist in v11) for column alignment.

cli-completion-command: chimera completion bash|zsh|fish outputs shell completion scripts as hardcoded template literals in completion.ts. Bash uses compgen, zsh uses _describe with typeset arrays, fish uses complete -c with -n predicates. Backslash-escape only ${} in template literals — plain $@ does not need escaping (ESLint no-useless-escape will flag it).

cloudmap-tools-tier-0-core: Cloud Map discovery tools (discover_infrastructure, get_service_instances, get_namespace_summary) must be registered at tier 0 in gateway_config.py so all agent tiers have infrastructure self-awareness regardless of subscription.

cloudmap-discovery-tools-tier0: Cloud Map discovery tools (discover_infrastructure, get_service_instances, get_namespace_summary) registered at tier 0 in gateway_config.py _TOOL_TIER_REGISTRY. Tier 0 = available to all agents regardless of subscription tier. Tools use boto3.client('servicediscovery') with paginated list_namespaces/list_services/list_instances. Default namespace from CHIMERA_ENV_NAME env var.

description: commander-hidehelp-not-on-command: .hideHelp() in Commander v11 exists on Option objects only, not on Command. Calling program.command('x').hideHelp() throws TypeError at runtime.
resolution: Use program.command('x', { hidden: true }) to hide a subcommand from help output.

content: chimera-destroy-all-15-stacks: STACK_DESTROY_ORDER must include all 15 stacks (Frontend, Discovery, GatewayRegistration, Email, Evolution, SkillPipeline, TenantOnboarding, Chat, Orchestration, Observability, Api, Pipeline, Security, Data, Network). Pre-delete steps required: disable DDB deletion protection (UpdateTableCommand) before Data stack teardown; empty S3 buckets (ListObjectVersions + DeleteObjects) for Frontend/Email/Pipeline/Data stacks. CHIMERA_STACK_SUFFIXES in doctor.ts must stay in sync.

cli-deploy-cdk-quiet-flag: deployCdkStacks() takes quiet=true param; CDK CloudFormation events are suppressed with .quiet() by default. Only show CDK output when --monitor flag is active (pass quiet=!options.monitor at call site).

cli-monitor-codepipeline: chimera monitor watches CodePipeline (GetPipelineStateCommand + ListPipelineExecutionsCommand) by default, not CloudFormation. Shows Source/Build/DockerBuild/Deploy stage transitions with per-action status. --stack flag retains CFN monitoring as fallback. ListPipelineExecutionsCommand(maxResults:1) gives overall execution status (Succeeded/Failed/InProgress); GetPipelineStateCommand gives per-stage/action detail.

cli-status-pipeline-always-shown: chimera status always fetches and displays CodePipeline execution status (no --pipeline flag required). getPipelineStatus uses ListPipelineExecutionsCommand(maxResults:1) for overall execution status — NOT GetPipelineStateCommand (which only gives per-stage status, not the overall execution status).

chimera-pipeline-name-distinction: The CodePipeline resource is named 'chimera-deploy-${env}' (lowercase, set in pipeline-stack.ts:1068 via pipelineName prop). The CloudFormation STACK that wraps it is named 'Chimera-${env}-Pipeline'. CLI commands must use the resource name (chimera-deploy-*) when calling CodePipeline SDK APIs like GetPipelineState or ListPipelineExecutions.

cli-pipeline-name-convention: CodePipeline resource name is chimera-deploy-${env} (lowercase, hyphenated). The CloudFormation STACK name is Chimera-${env}-Pipeline (PascalCase). CLI commands using CodePipeline SDK (GetPipelineStateCommand, ListPipelineExecutionsCommand, ListActionExecutionsCommand) must use the pipeline resource name, not the stack name. Source of truth: infra/lib/pipeline-stack.ts pipelineName property.

cli-deploy-quiet-cdk: deployCdkStacks() in deploy.ts accepts quiet param (default true) to suppress CDK CloudFormation event output via Bun.$.quiet(). Only shows CDK events when --monitor flag is passed (quiet=false). Pattern: const proc = Bun.$`cmd`; await (quiet ? proc.quiet() : proc);

cli-monitor-codepipeline-default: chimera monitor now watches CodePipeline execution stages by default (GetPipelineStateCommand + ListPipelineExecutionsCommand, polling every 10s). Shows stage-by-stage progress with action details for active/failed stages. --stack flag falls back to CloudFormation event monitoring via cf-monitor.ts. Pipeline name: chimera-deploy-${env}.

description: Docker Build pipeline: '/packages/chat-gateway/public': not found — stale COPY in Dockerfile after public/ dir was removed in FrontendStack refactor (6f9b306). Issue was misdiagnosed as Docker Hub rate limit.
resolution: Remove COPY packages/chat-gateway/public ./public from chat-gateway Dockerfile. When removing static asset directories, always update all Dockerfile COPY references in the same commit.

content: bun-binary-asset-embedding: Compiled Bun binaries must use Bun.file(new URL('./asset', import.meta.url)).text() to embed static assets. readFileSync(import.meta.dir, ...) fails at runtime because the file path doesn't exist in the compiled binary context.

chat-gateway-messages-format: The chat-gateway /chat/stream endpoint expects OpenAI-compatible format: {messages:[{role:'user',content:'...',}], sessionId?:'...'} — NOT the flat {message:'...'} field. Sending the wrong format returns EMPTY_MESSAGES 400 error.

bun-binary-html-static-import: In compiled Bun binaries, import HTML files as string with 'import html from "./file.html" with { type: "text" }' at module top level. Bun.file(new URL(...)) approach is insufficient — import.meta.url resolves to bunfs:// path at runtime and the file is not accessible. TypeScript will infer HTMLBundle type; cast with 'as unknown as string' at use site. Also add '*.html' module declaration override if gitignore allows .d.ts files.

chat-sse-parser-dsp-format: CLI chat SSE parsers (chat.ts + useChat.ts streamChatResponse) must map Vercel DSP events: type:'text-delta'.delta → token content, type:'finish' → done. Server emits DSP, not {type:'token',content}. Both classic REPL and TUI useChat hook have independent parsers that both need updating.

bedrock-default-model-inference-profile: Default BEDROCK_MODEL_ID must be an inference profile (us.anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022-v2:0), not a legacy on-demand model ID. Legacy IDs like 'anthropic.claude-3-sonnet-20240229-v1:0' throw ResourceNotFoundException when called without BEDROCK_MODEL_ID env var set.

amplify-v6-signin-nextstep-pattern: signIn() returns { isSignedIn, nextStep.signInStep }. Handle CONFIRM_SIGN_IN_WITH_TOTP_CODE, CONFIRM_SIGN_IN_WITH_SMS_CODE, CONTINUE_SIGN_IN_WITH_TOTP_SETUP, CONFIRM_SIGN_IN_WITH_NEW_PASSWORD_REQUIRED, CONFIRM_SIGN_UP. All challenges including new-password and TOTP setup code verification use confirmSignIn({ challengeResponse }). fetchAuthSession() gives access/id tokens post-auth but not refresh token directly in v6.

cli-browser-auth-callback-security: Web login page reads ?callback= query param. After auth, POSTs { access_token, id_token, refresh_token: '', expires_in: 3600 } to callback URL only if it matches /^https?:\/\/(localhost|127\.0\.0\.1)(:\d+)?\// — prevents token exfiltration to arbitrary URLs. Shows 'you can close this window' success view on success. No callback navigates to /dashboard.

cli-browser-login-callback-pattern: chimera login --browser opens deployed frontend URL (frontend_url from chimera.toml [endpoints]) with ?callback=http://localhost:9999/auth/callback. CLI runs a CORS-enabled callback server; deployed React app POSTs tokens after Cognito auth. Falls back to terminal login if frontend_url not set. browser-login.html removed from CLI binary.

cli-browser-server-cors-pattern: browser-server.ts CORS setup for localhost callback: allowedOrigin = new URL(frontendUrl).origin; OPTIONS handler returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin/Methods/Headers; POST handler response includes Access-Control-Allow-Origin. Tied to specific deployed origin, not wildcard.

model-router-ddb-init-antipattern: model-router.ts creates module-level DynamoDB singleton at import time, causing 6 static-routing unit tests to fail even though they never touch DynamoDB. Fix: lazy-initialize the client inside functions that need it, or make it injectable.

roadmap-stack-count-stale: docs/ROADMAP.md claims 14 CDK stacks but 15 exist in infra/lib/ as of 2026-03-31 (gateway-registration-stack.ts added post-2026-03-26). Always verify stack count against infra/lib/*-stack.ts, not ROADMAP.

cli-pipeline-test-stage-source-input: CodePipeline Test stage must use sourceOutput (not buildOutput) as primary CodeBuildAction input. buildOutput only contains CDK synth artifacts and lacks package.json/bun.lockb needed for bun install. dockerOutput stays as extraInput.

bun-test-beforeall-timeout: bun test default timeout is 5s, which is too short for CDK PipelineStack beforeAll synthesis (4 Lambda inline functions + Step Functions). Pass 120_000ms as second arg to all beforeAll() hooks in CDK tests, otherwise all tests in the describe block are silently skipped.

Project audit 2026-04-01: 15 CDK stacks, 16 CLI commands, 30 ADRs, 2146/2186 tests passing. 31 failures cluster into 4 fixable categories: E2E infra dependency, ChatMessage jsdom setup, ModelRouter AWS SDK mock missing, git subprocess test isolation.

chat-gateway agent-system-prompt-tools-wiring: chat.ts uses lazy singleton GatewayToolDiscovery (ToolRegistry + ToolLoader) to load tier-gated AWS tools per request; createAgent() receives loadedTools + tier from tenantContext. ToolRegistry init requires AWS_ACCOUNT_ID, CHIMERA_TENANT_ROLE_PATTERN, CHIMERA_CONFIG_AGGREGATOR_NAME env vars; falls back to no tools if unset. StrandsTool[] maps directly to AgentConfig.loadedTools[].

chimera-default-system-prompt: DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT in packages/core/src/agent/prompt.ts identifies agent as Chimera AWS agent with EC2/S3/Lambda/DDB; lists tier-gated tool categories; uses {{tenantId}} and {{sessionId}} template vars rendered by ChimeraAgent.runReActLoop().

e2e-rune2e-gate: E2E and integration tests that need live AWS infra must check process.env.RUN_E2E at the top of each describe block — if not set, register a test.skip and return. This is the unified gate for all live-infra tests (tests/e2e/ and tests/integration/). CI does not set RUN_E2E so tests skip cleanly instead of failing.

agentcore-runtime-6-method-api: AgentCoreRuntime class in packages/core/src/runtime/agentcore-runtime.ts exposes 10 methods: createSession, resumeSession, terminateSession, storeMemory, retrieveMemory, queryMemory (existing placeholders) + invokeAgent, getSessionHistory, deleteSession, updateMemory (chimera-3a32 additions). invokeAgent/getSessionHistory throw 'not yet implemented'. deleteSession delegates to terminateSession. updateMemory routes to per-operation methods. SDK: @aws-sdk/client-bedrock-agent-runtime is the target (InvokeAgentCommand, GetSessionCommand, DeleteSessionCommand). All 31 unit tests pass.

chimera-init-model-section: chimera init prompts for Bedrock model (inference profile), prompt caching, and max context tokens. Saves to [model] section in chimera.toml with model_id, prompt_caching, max_tokens, temperature=0.7. Region prefix (us/eu/ap) derived from chosen AWS region. getRegionInferencePrefix() and getDefaultModelId() exported for testability.

bedrock-prompt-caching-env-var: chat-gateway BedrockConfig now includes promptCaching field. BEDROCK_PROMPT_CACHING=true env var enables it. Default model updated from claude-3-5-sonnet to claude-sonnet-4-6 inference profile (us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6-v1:0). Default max tokens updated from 4096 to 200000 to match extended context capability.

orchestrator-narrow-interface-di: AgentOrchestrator accepts injectable OrchestratorSQSClient/DDBClient/EventBridgeClient interfaces via config.clients. Plain mock objects satisfy these in tests. Real adapters use module-level singletons (per-region Map, same pattern as event-bus.ts). AgentCore Runtime createAgentRuntime remains a stub until AgentCore SDK is available.

ddb-update-expression-reserved-keyword: DynamoDB UpdateExpression for 'status' field requires ExpressionAttributeNames alias (#s = :status) because 'status' is a reserved word in DynamoDB. Use ExpressionAttributeNames whenever updating fields with common names (status, name, type, role, etc.).

sqs-queue-creation-two-step: Creating an SQS queue with DLQ requires two SQS calls: (1) CreateQueueCommand for DLQ, (2) GetQueueAttributesCommand to get DLQ ARN, (3) CreateQueueCommand for main queue with RedrivePolicy referencing dlqArn. QueueArn is not returned by CreateQueue — must be fetched separately.

skill-provider-interface: SkillProvider (skills/ layer) vs ToolProvider (tools/ layer) are separate abstractions. SkillProvider implements discoverSkills(), loadSkill(name), executeSkill(name, input) for skill-source backends (MCP gateway, SKILL.md registry). ToolProvider implements initialize(), listTools(), invoke(), cleanup() for tool-level invocation within an installed skill. Each provider class in skills/ implements both interfaces. SkillLoader in tools/ creates lightweight inline ToolProvider objects without depending on SkillProvider.

api-stack-lambda-integration-pattern: ApiStack accepts optional tenantsTable/sessionsTable/skillsTable props. When provided, 5 Lambda handlers (tenants, sessions, skills, agents, chat) are created with LambdaIntegration(proxy=true). When omitted (unit tests), falls back to 501 MockIntegration. addApiMethod() helper accepts optional lambdaFn param — uses LambdaIntegration when present, MockIntegration otherwise. Agents stored in tenants table SK=AGENT#{id}; installed skills stored in skills table with PK=TENANT#{tenantId} SK=SKILL#{id}.

api-handler-tenant-isolation: All management API Lambda handlers validate caller's JWT custom:tenant_id claim against the {tenantId} path parameter. Platform admin (chimera-platform tenant) may bypass. Claims available at event.requestContext.authorizer.claims in Cognito authorizer. Pattern: validateTenantAccess(event, pathTenantId) exported for unit testing.

hitl-gateway-ddb-pattern: HITLGateway uses narrow HITLDDBClient injectable interface (get/put plain objects) with lazy module-level singleton fallback. Schema: PK=TENANT#{tenantId}, SK=APPROVAL#{requestId} on chimera-sessions table. Status lifecycle: pending -> approved|denied. saveApprovalRequest(), getApprovalStatus(), resolveApproval() are the 3 public DDB methods.

cherry-pick-orphaned-commits: When builder worktrees are cleaned before merge, commits remain accessible via git cherry-pick using their SHA. Cherry-pick fix commits before doc commits to maintain logical dependency order.

bun-module-singleton-tdc: Module-level AWS SDK singletons (const ddbClient = new DynamoDBClient({})) cause TDZ errors when mock.module() is used in sibling test files — bun re-evaluates the module with the mock in scope. Fix: lazy getter pattern (create client on first use, not at module init).

code-defender-git-test-isolation: System-level core.hooksPath=/usr/local/amazon/var/git-defender/hooks causes git commit to fail silently in test temp repos. Fix: git -C tmpDir config core.hooksPath /dev/null immediately after git init in beforeEach.

bun-test-jsdom-setup: @testing-library/react tests using vitest imports fail in bun test because document is undefined. Fix: add beforeAll() with JSDOM setup — import jsdom, create dom instance, assign window/document/HTMLElement etc to globalThis.
e2e-test-gating-pattern: E2E tests that require live AWS infra should use describe.if(process.env.RUN_E2E === '1') to prevent CI failures on offline runs.

e2e-test-describe-if-pattern: Use describe.if(process.env.RUN_E2E === '1') for E2E test gating in bun, not manual if(!env){test.skip();return} guard inside describe block. The describe.if() pattern is cleaner and framework-native.

codebuild-bun-path-export-per-phase: In CodeBuild, use 'export PATH=$HOME/.bun/bin:$PATH' at the start of each phase that calls bun (pre_build, build). Do NOT use '. $HOME/.bun/env' — the env file may not exist on Standard 7.0 images, causing exit status 2. The PATH export is idempotent and always works since the bun binary is in .bun/bin after install.

boto3-elbv2-service-name: In Lambda Python 3.12 runtime, use boto3.client('elbv2') not boto3.client('elasticloadbalancingv2'). The current Lambda botocore only recognizes 'elbv2' as a valid ELBv2 service name — 'elasticloadbalancingv2' raises UnknownServiceError at Lambda init time, failing before any handler code runs.

tests-package-json-paths-relative: In tests/package.json scripts, test paths must be relative to tests/ cwd (e.g., 'bun test integration'), NOT 'bun test tests/integration'. The latter resolves to tests/tests/integration which doesn't exist, causing exit status 1 in CodeBuild.
